page_title: Missouri Termination Law and At-Will Exceptions 2026 | Teamed
meta_description: Missouri at-will employment 2026: narrow Fleshner public-policy exception, Service Letter Statute (RSMo § 290.140), MHRA 6-employee threshold, final pay due at separation on involuntary dismissal.
target_keywords: missouri human rights act employee threshold

flags.description: RSMo § 290.140 Service Letter Statute — 90-day employee threshold and 'true reason for dismissal' requirement are provisional facts. The statute text at revisor.mo.gov must be confirmed before any client-facing use. False service letter liability (actual + punitive damages) is a significant legal claim; verify the exact language of the statute before publishing.
flags.action: Verify RSMo § 290.140 at https://revisor.mo.gov/main/OneSection.aspx?section=290.140 — confirm 90-day threshold, mandatory elements, and damages exposure.

flags.description: RSMo § 290.110 penalty wages — the 7-day certified-mail demand window before 60 days of additional wages accrue is a provisional fact from the compliance cache. Exact statutory language must be verified before publishing.
flags.action: Verify at https://revisor.mo.gov/main/OneSection.aspx?section=290.110 — confirm certified mail demand requirement, 7-day window, and 60-day penalty cap.

flags.description: Fleshner v. Pepose Vision Institute, 304 S.W.3d 81 (Mo. banc 2010) — the four public-policy exception categories are stated as confirmed by this case. Case citation should be verified against the Missouri Supreme Court's published opinion to confirm the exact four-category framework is correctly characterised.
flags.action: Confirm the four Fleshner categories against the published opinion text before client use.

flags.description: Missouri Human Rights Act 6-employee threshold (RSMo § 213.010) is provisional. Verify the current statutory definition of 'employer' under MHRA for the threshold applied to termination claims.
